name: Lemon-Pecan Torte
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F. Grease a 9-inch springform pan; dust with matzoh meal.
2. Prepare Torte: Whisk yolks and sugar in a large bowl until blended. Stir in pecans and lemon rind. Beat egg whites and lemon juice in a large bowl until stiff but not dry peaks form. Stir one-fourth of the whites into the yolk mixture. Fold in the remaining whites until blended. Scrape batter into the prepared pan.
3. Bake at 325°F for 1 hour or until firm in center to touch. Cool on a wire rack for 15 minutes. The cake will sink slightly in the center.
4. Meanwhile, prepare Glaze: Combine yolk, lemon juice, sugar, and margarine in a small saucepan. Bring to simmering over medium heat, whisking constantly. Remove from heat, stir in rind.
5. Poke holes in the top of the cake with a long skewer. With the cake still in the springform pan, spoon glaze over it. Let stand a few minutes for the glaze to seep in. Carefully remove the cake from the pan by running a thin knife around the rim; then carefully remove the sides. Garnish with lemon peel, if desired.
